summary: American modernist poet. Best known for The Bridge (epic poem using Brooklyn Bridge as symbol, sections include "Powhatan's Daughter" and a Columbus monologue) and White Buildings (containing "Voyages" and "At Melville's Tomb"). Committed suicide by jumping into the Gulf of Mexico from the ship Orizaba in 1932.

works.name: The Bridge
works.description: Epic poem using the Brooklyn Bridge as central symbol. Subtitled "The Evening Redness of the West."

clues.clue: Epic poem using Brooklyn Bridge as symbol — ends with plea "of the curveship lend a myth to God"
examples: of the curveship lend a myth to God

clues.clue: "Powhatan's Daughter" section contains invocation "O Appalachian Spring!" and Rip Van Winkle sweeping a tenement
Columbus monologue thanking the Virgin Mary for safe passage
examples: a dramatic monologue in which Columbus thanks the Virgin Mary

clues.clue: Title subject can "condense eternity" — "we have seen night lifted in thine arms"
Melvin Tolson's Libretto for the Republic of Liberia modeled on it
examples: Melvin Tolson's Libretto for the Republic of Liberia is modeled on a long poem by this author

works.name: White Buildings
works.description: First poetry collection (1926), introduced by Allen Tate. Contains "Voyages" and "At Melville's Tomb."

clues.clue: Contains "Voyages" — six love poems for sailor Emil Opffer; ocean is "a great wink of eternity"
examples: a sequence of six love poems for the sailor Emil Opffer
described the ocean as a 'great wink of eternity'

clues.clue: "At Melville's Tomb" — "The dice of drowned men's bones he saw bequeath / An embassy"; "High in the azure steeps / Monody shall not wake the mariner"
examples: The dice of drowned men's bones he saw bequeath / An embassy
High in the azure steeps / Monody shall not wake the mariner
Allen Tate introduced his debut collection

clues.clue: Also contains "Chaplinesque" — "we make our meek adjustments"
Committed suicide by jumping from ship Orizaba into Gulf of Mexico (1932) — after being attacked for sexual advances toward crew
examples: jumped into the Gulf of Mexico to commit suicide
occurred shortly after departing Vera Cruz with Peggy Cowley

clues.clue: Yvor Winters connected his suicide to "doctrine of Emerson and Whitman" in In Defense of Reason
examples: connected to the 'doctrine of Emerson and Whitman'

clues.clue: William H. Gass juxtaposed his death with his mother's in "The Doomed in Their Sinking"
examples: Gass juxtaposed this event with the slow, spiteful death of his mother

clues.clue: "O Carib Isle" — "You have given me the shell, Satan"; sun "exploded in the sea"
examples: declares 'You have given me the shell, Satan'

comprehensive_summary: Hart Crane is an American modernist poet best known for The Bridge, an epic poem using the Brooklyn Bridge as its central symbol. The poem ends with the plea "of the curveship lend a myth to God" and contains the section "Powhatan's Daughter" (with the invocation "O Appalachian Spring!" and Rip Van Winkle sweeping a tenement) and a Columbus dramatic monologue. White Buildings (1926), introduced by Allen Tate, contains "Voyages" (six love poems for sailor Emil Opffer describing the ocean as "a great wink of eternity") and "At Melville's Tomb" ("The dice of drowned men's bones he saw bequeath / An embassy"). Crane committed suicide in 1932 by jumping from the ship Orizaba into the Gulf of Mexico after being attacked for making sexual advances toward crew members.
